evals.name: sse-vs-websockets
evals.prompt: Write a TDR for choosing between Server-Sent Events and WebSockets for a live notifications feed in our dashboard. Stack is FastAPI on the backend and Next.js on the frontend. I want it Confluence-paste-ready so the team can review it.
evals.expected_output: A decision-first TDR recommending one option with a clear rationale; canonical section skeleton; a decision log with status lozenges; an Alternatives section comparing SSE vs WebSockets; a gap audit + blocker sub-specs (full rigor). Dual output: a Mermaid master (-design.md) and a Confluence-paste-safe ASCII copy (-confluence.md) that passes the paste-safety lint.
assertions: Produces two files: a Mermaid master ending in -design.md AND a Confluence ASCII copy ending in -confluence.md
The -confluence.md file contains no ```mermaid blocks, no <details>/<summary>, and no table rows that start with '>' (passes paste_safety_lint.py)
Contains a Decision log section (table with a Status column using lozenges like DECIDED/PROPOSED)
Contains an Alternatives section that explicitly compares SSE vs WebSockets and states why the non-chosen option was rejected
Contains a gap audit section (low-level items still to specify, prioritised) AND a sub-specs section giving concrete contracts for the blockers
Any backend contract/endpoint/field it states is either grounded or explicitly marked (confirm) since it could not read the real repo

evals.name: redis-session-store
evals.prompt: We're moving interview session storage from in-memory to Redis. Document this as a design record — the problem and why now, the alternatives we passed on (e.g. sticky sessions, DynamoDB), and a clear list of what's still unspecified before an engineer can start building.
evals.expected_output: A TDR with a crisp decision (Redis), a decision log, an alternatives comparison (in-memory/sticky vs DynamoDB vs Redis), and a strong gap audit listing the still-unspecified low-level details, with concrete sub-specs for the blocking ones. Dual output, Confluence-paste-safe ASCII copy passes the lint.
assertions: Produces two files: a Mermaid master ending in -design.md AND a Confluence ASCII copy ending in -confluence.md
The -confluence.md file passes paste_safety_lint.py (no mermaid fences, no <details>, no blockquoted tables)
Contains a Decision log and an Alternatives comparison covering at least two rejected options
Contains a gap audit prioritising what is still unspecified before build, plus concrete sub-specs (e.g. key schema, TTL, serialization) for the blockers
States proposed contracts (key naming, TTL, data shape) and marks them (confirm) rather than asserting unverified facts
